_note: SCAFFOLD — enabled:false. The candidate-list-table URL is an HTML page, not a CSV export; confirm the real machine-readable export (CSV/XLSX or ECHA API) and its exact column headers, then write/validate an HTML-table or CSV parser before enabling. Do NOT enable against a guessed schema (correctness).

WIRED, enabled:false. EurLexHtmlParser now exists and is tested against real Cellar OJ XHTML. Enabling is BLOCKED for correctness: the only machine-reachable Cellar endpoint serves the BASE act only (CELEX 32006R1907), which OMITS the many later Annex XVII amendments — an incomplete restricted-substances list. Enabling requires a CONSOLIDATED source (solve the eur-lex.europa.eu JS-challenge fetch for consolidated HTML, or an ECHA data feed); only then flip enabled:true.

documents.title: Directive 2011/65/EU (RoHS) base act — Annex II (Restricted substances)

_note: WIRED, enabled:false. EurLexHtmlParser now exists and is tested against real Cellar OJ XHTML. Enabling is BLOCKED for correctness: the only machine-reachable Cellar endpoint serves the BASE act only (CELEX 32011L0065), and its Annex II OMITS the four phthalates (DEHP, BBP, DBP, DIBP) added by Delegated Directive (EU) 2015/863 — the base Annex II lists the six original substances by name with no CAS entries, so enabling here would screen against an incomplete restriction list. Enabling requires a CONSOLIDATED source (solve the eur-lex.europa.eu JS-challenge fetch for consolidated HTML, or an ECHA data feed); only then flip enabled:true.

regulation: US FDA Food Additive & Food-Contact Regulations (21 CFR)
_note: ENABLED — parses the eCFR versioner /versions JSON (verified schema: content_versions[].{identifier,amendment_date,name,part,title,type,removed}). Emits one citable chunk per current section (parts 170,175,176,177,178) carrying its authoritative last-amended date for change detection. This indexes section-level metadata + amendment dates, not full CFR text; full-text ingestion via /full/{date}/title-21.xml is a future enhancement pending a validated CFR-XML parser.

ENABLED — same verified EcfrParser as FDA. No parts filter is set (Title 40 is large), so the first sync will exceed the circuit-breaker diff threshold and be HELD for R.E. sign-off — the intended safe behavior for a large new source. Scope it by adding parserConfig.parts (the EPA parts relevant to marker screening) once the R.E. confirms them.

SCAFFOLD — enabled:false. This is an HTML status page with no stable machine-readable schema; it tracks the evolving PFAS universal-restriction dossier rather than a fixed substance list. Confirm a structured source (or accept a curated summary) and build a validated parser before enabling.
